display_name: Northern Plains Tribal Health Region A

sources.population: U.S. Census Bureau. ACS 2020-2024 5-Year Estimates. Northern Plains tribal population Reservation total population ~8,250.
sources.ethnicity: U.S. Census Bureau ACS 2020-2024. Northern Plains tribal population Reservation: American Indian/Alaska Native >85% of population.
sources.sex_ratio: U.S. Census Bureau ACS 2020-2024. Female 51.2% on Northern Plains tribal population Reservation.
sources.diabetes_prevalence: Dai J et al. Trends in anti-diabetic medication use among AI/AN Peoples 2009-2013. Diabetes Obes Metab. 2024;27(1):328-337. doi:10.1111/dom.16021. AI/AN diabetes prevalence 13.6% nationally (2019-2021), nearly twice non-Hispanic White adults (6.9%).
sources.hypertension_prevalence: Jolly SE et al. Prevalence of Hypertension in Western Alaska Native People: WATCH Study. J Clin Hypertens. 2015;17(10):812-818. doi:10.1111/jch.12483. Strong Heart Study North/South Dakota AI/AN HTN prevalence ~27% at baseline.
sources.dm_htn_comorbidity: Walls ML et al. Together Overcoming Diabetes Study. J Diabetes Res. 2025. doi:10.1155/jdr/6591307. 77.9% of AI/AN adults with T2D also have hypertension across 14 IHS units.
sources.ckd_comorbidity: Nephrology Mini Orals. Nephrology. 2021;26(S2):17-32. doi:10.1111/nep.13930. DM+HTN strongly associated with CKD in indigenous communities.
sources.sepsis_social_determinants: Ardabili AK et al. Social Determinants of Sepsis Mortality in the US. World Med Health Policy. 2025;17(4):823-830. doi:10.1002/wmh3.70043. Higher ADI scores and lower income associated with increased sepsis mortality.
sources.afebrile_elderly: Gavazzi G, Krause KH. Ageing and infection. Lancet Infect Dis. 2002;2(11):659-666. doi:10.1016/S1473-3099(02)00437-1.
sources.ihs_workforce: Brockie T et al. Indigenous nursing leadership. J Clin Nurs. 2021;32(3-4):610-624. doi:10.1111/jocn.15801. IHS nurse vacancy rate 23-40%. Context for access limitations.

calibration_notes: AI/AN DM prevalence 13.6% nationally — significantly higher than US average of ~8.7% (ADA 2024). Engine uses US-baseline comorbidity generator; AI/AN-specific prevalence not yet applied as regional adjustment. Documented gap.
HTN prevalence ~27% in ND AI/AN (Strong Heart Study baseline) vs US average ~45% (AHA 2023). This population has LOWER HTN than US average but HIGHER diabetes burden.
77.9% of AI/AN adults with T2D also have HTN — when DM is present, HTN co-occurrence is extremely high.
CKD elevated due to high DM+HTN comorbidity burden in this population.
Rural flag = True. Pneumonia weighted higher as sepsis infection source (rural presentation pattern).
IHS access limitations: nurse vacancy rate 23-40%. This cohort represents patients who did reach ICU care — not population-representative.

continuous_vars._source: IHS Trends in Indian Health 2014-2015; NHANES AI/AN oversampling; Strong Heart Study ND/SD data.

hba1c.source: IHS data; AI/AN DM prevalence 13.6% (Dai 2024); higher HbA1c burden vs US mean

bmi.source: BRFSS AI/AN oversampling; Strong Heart Study. Higher obesity burden in AI/AN populations.

systolic_bp.source: Strong Heart Study ND/SD AI/AN; Jolly 2015 WATCH Study; HTN estimated 27-40% AI/AN

creatinine.source: NHANES AI/AN CKD data; higher CKD burden from DM+HTN comorbidity; Nephrology 2021

egfr.source: IHS CKD surveillance; CKD-EPI 2021; elevated CKD stage 3+ in AI/AN DM+HTN population

temporal_trends._source: IHS trend data; CDC AI/AN surveillance; CMS Part D AI/AN utilization; BRFSS AI/AN oversampling

disease_prevalence_shift._unit: absolute prevalence change (fraction) at 6-month drift interval

prescription_shift._unit: relative change fraction at 6-month drift interval

6.source: IHS trends 2024; CDC AI/AN Diabetes Surveillance; BRFSS AI/AN 2022

disease_prevalence_shift._unit: absolute prevalence change (fraction) at 12-month drift interval

prescription_shift._unit: relative change fraction at 12-month drift interval

12.source: IHS trends 2024; CDC AI/AN Diabetes Surveillance; BRFSS AI/AN 2022

disease_prevalence_shift._unit: absolute prevalence change (fraction) at 18-month drift interval

prescription_shift._unit: relative change fraction at 18-month drift interval

18.source: IHS trends 2024; CDC AI/AN Diabetes Surveillance; BRFSS AI/AN 2022
